programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: After a career criminal committed a vehicular homicide in New Orleans, the FBI tracked him through his habits.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: A teenager was shot to death on a hunting ground in Virginia. His death was initially considered an accident but investigators soon suspected foul play. Years later, a man contacted the FBI with a complex story of conspiracy and murder-for-hire.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: The decapitation of an African-American male led the Miami-Dade police and the FBI along a trail of murder.

programme.title: The FBI Files

programme.title: The FBI Files

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: A cross-country crime spree over a Labor Day weekend terrorized the nation. In a small Ohio community, an elderly woman and teenage boy disappeared within hours of one another. The police suspected the cases were linked.

programme.title: The FBI Files

programme.desc: In 1993, an elderly man was found suffocated in his home with his checkbook, credit cards and vehicle stolen.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1985, a Lebanese terrorist and his four accomplices hijacked Royal Jordanian Flight 405 before taking off.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In June 1973, a young girl was abducted from a state park while camping with her family. A criminal profiler predicted that the kidnapper would contact the girl's family on the one-year anniversary of her abduction.

programme.title: The FBI Files

programme.desc: The story of one of the most prolific serial killers in Canadian history, dubbed The Pig Farmer Killer.

programme.desc: A killer tried to incinerate and destroy everything that could link him to his crime. But in doing so, he inadvertently created new forensic evidence, evidence which came to light with a technique never before used in a criminal investigation.

programme.desc: A woman was shot to death in her Connecticut driveway. Now police must determine if love had turned deadly.

programme.desc: Scott Dunn was missing and when the police sprayed his bedroom with Luminol, a scene of horrific violence emerged. Now investigators faced a daunting task.

programme.desc: The medical examiner ruled a death an accident, but the detectives thought the evidence indicated otherwise.

programme.desc: The decomposed body of a young woman was discovered in a Bakersfield irrigation canal. If there was trace evidence, it had been washed away.

programme.desc: Responding to a 2008 garage fire, Illinois emergency response discovered a man crushed beneath a truck.

programme.title: Forensic Files

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1995, the founder of American Atheists disappeared with one of her sons, her granddaughter and $500,000 in gold coins. The police investigated several suspects, including David R. Waters, who worked for American Atheists.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A man tells police he shot an intruder who killed his wife. Forensic evidence disproves that story later on.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1984, California firefighters battled ten arson fires in three weeks. When cigarettes and a scrap of paper connected the case to similar fires further north, the hunt was on for a dangerous pyromaniac.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1994, Shannon Melendi disappeared while at Emory University. Her disappearance remained a mystery for ten years, until new scientific testing cast a different light on Colvin "Butch" Hinton.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 2001, Ginger Hayes and her infant son Nicholas were abducted during a carjacking and the crime had been reported by a witness within minutes of occurrence.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: British detectives worked with a pioneering scientist to solve crimes of sexual assault and serial murder. This 1986 case marked the first time DNA was used as evidence in a court of law.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A woman who suffered from mental illness is found dead. Was it due to natural causes, suicide or murder?

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Bombings are difficult to solve, because the perpetrator isn't usually at the scene, and the evidence goes up in smoke. In this case pieces of plastic the size of grains of sand hold the key to a man's murder.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When an 11-year-old girl disappeared from a small town in a remote area of Alaska, investigators wondered if she'd been attacked by a bear or become lost in the dense woods.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A three-year-old develops a respiratory condition. His father has Alzheimers. An investigator reveals that their ill health is due to mold in their modern home.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The body of 16-year-old Leanne Tiernan was discovered nine months after she disappeared. Forensic scientists found clues that painted a virtual portrait of her killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: On October 15, 1985, two bomb explosions rocked Salt Lake City and resulted in two deaths. A third explosion occurred the next day. This time, the victim was injured but survived.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A bullet-riddled car, a missing driver, and no witnesses, an ambush or a random attack; the clue was something so tiny, it was measured in millionths of a meter.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Police are able to create a composite sketch of a man burnt beyond recognition. But tracking the killer is more challenging.
